document.name: preview-dev-gan
document.title: In-preview GAN dev loop: edit the live BFF (HMR) ↔ Playwright visual critic
document.summary: Runs INSIDE a Tier-2 vcluster preview. enter_dev_mode replaces the preview's prod BFF pod with a hot-reload dev-server Sandbox that adopts the preview's Service (so the preview URL serves live edits — P1 'preview-native' mode). Then a generator↔critic GAN loop: the generator edits /sandbox/work/repo/src and runs ./sync.sh to push changes to the live preview (vite HMR, ~seconds), and an independent Playwright-MCP visual critic drives a real browser against the in-cluster preview URL, screenshots the rendered app, and grades it — looping until accepted or the iteration cap. Generator + critic share the per-execution JuiceFS /sandbox/work (interactive-cli family). See docs/agentic-deploy-inspect-loop.md + docs/generator-critic-multi-agent.md.

intent.title: Change request
intent.default: Redesign the PUBLIC sign-in page — the page served at / for a logged-out visitor (its Svelte component lives under src/routes, e.g. the login/(auth) route, plus src/app.css). Give it a distinctive, polished, cohesive look: strong visual hierarchy for the product name, a clearly prominent primary 'Sign in' action, a deliberate color + spacing system, and one memorable accent. Do NOT touch authenticated/dashboard routes. Keep the sign-in form working.
intent.description: What the generator changes in the live BFF UI (edits land under src/ and hot-reload onto the preview).

service.title: Service
service.description: The preview service whose pod is replaced with a dev server (P1 preview-native; workflow-builder = the BFF).

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: Drives Playwright MCP against the live preview URL + grades the UI. interactive-cli family (in-pod Chromium).

intent.title: Change request
intent.default: Redesign the PUBLIC sign-in page — the page served at / for a logged-out visitor (its Svelte component lives under src/routes, e.g. the login/(auth) route, plus src/app.css). Give it a distinctive, polished, cohesive look: strong visual hierarchy for the product name, a clearly prominent primary 'Sign in' action, a deliberate color + spacing system, and one memorable accent. Do NOT touch authenticated/dashboard routes. Keep the sign-in form working.

artifacts.from: ${ { service: (.data.service // ""), url: (.data.url // ""), browseUrl: (.data.browseUrl // ""), syncUrl: (.data.syncUrl // ""), ready: (.data.ready // false) } }
artifacts.title: Preview-native dev mode

with.command:
${ "export REPOURL=" + ((.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/workflow-builder") | @sh) + "; export REF=" + ((.trigger.repoRef // "main") | @sh) + "; export SUBDIR=" + ((.enter_dev_mode.repoSubdir // ".") | @sh) + "; export PATHS=" + (((.enter_dev_mode.syncPaths // ["src"]) | join(" ")) | @sh) + "; export SYNCURL=" + ((.enter_dev_mode.syncUrl // "") | @sh) + ";" + "
set -e
cd /sandbox/work
rm -rf repo
git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --branch "$REF" https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/$REPOURL.git repo
printf 'SUBDIR=%s\nPATHS="%s"\nSYNCURL=%s\n' "$SUBDIR" "$PATHS" "$SYNCURL" > /sandbox/work/.syncenv
cat > /sandbox/work/sync.sh <<'SH'
#!/bin/sh
set -e
. /sandbox/work/.syncenv
cd /sandbox/work/repo/$SUBDIR
SYNCPATHS=""; for p in $PATHS; do [ -e "$p" ] && SYNCPATHS="$SYNCPATHS $p"; done
tar -czf /tmp/sync.tgz $SYNCPATHS
curl -sS -X POST --data-binary @/tmp/sync.tgz -H 'content-type: application/gzip' "$SYNCURL"
echo
echo SYNCED
SH
chmod +x /sandbox/work/sync.sh
echo CLONED $(git -C repo rev-parse --short HEAD); echo '--- .syncenv ---'; cat /sandbox/work/.syncenv" }

${ "URL=" + ((.enter_dev_mode.url // "") | @sh) + "; echo prewarming $URL ...; for i in 1 2 3; do code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 240 \"$URL/\" 2>/dev/null); echo attempt $i -> $code; [ \"$code\" != 000 ] && break; sleep 3; done; echo PREWARMED" }

agentConfig.name: Generator (live BFF dev)
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR in a generator-critic loop refining a LIVE, hot-reloading microservice UI. The repo is cloned at /sandbox/work/repo. The TARGET is the PUBLIC sign-in page — the page a logged-out visitor sees at / (find its Svelte route under /sandbox/work/repo/src/routes, e.g. a login/(auth)/+page.svelte, plus shared styles in src/app.css). EDIT ONLY files under /sandbox/work/repo/src (only paths under there are synced). Do NOT touch authenticated/dashboard routes and do NOT break the sign-in form. After each batch of edits, run /sandbox/work/sync.sh to push to the live preview (vite HMR, a few seconds). Then VERIFY it took by curling the IN-CLUSTER preview URL from the prompt with a GENEROUS timeout (the dev server compiles SSR on first load — use `curl -s --max-time 240 <url>/`); confirm your change is in the returned HTML. An independent visual Critic reviews the RENDERED sign-in page each round and returns specific feedback — address it, make the most impactful improvement, run ./sync.sh, then end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Change request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the sign-in page") + "

The repo is cloned at /sandbox/work/repo; edit ONLY the PUBLIC sign-in page + styles under repo/" + (.enter_dev_mode.repoSubdir // ".") + "/src (the page served at / when logged out). After editing run /sandbox/work/sync.sh, then verify with `curl -s --max-time 240 " + (.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + "/` (the first render is slow — be patient). Do NOT curl the public URL " + (.enter_dev_mode.browseUrl // "") + ".

Your previous attempt summary:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(this is your first attempt)") + "

Critic feedback to address now:
" + (if .loop.last.critique then (.loop.last.critique.feedback // (.loop.last.critique | tojson)) else "(none yet — make your first improvement)" end) + "

Implement the change under repo/src, run ./sync.sh, confirm it is live, then end with a brief summary." }

agentConfig.name: Visual critic (Playwright)
agentConfig.instructions: You are an exacting, INDEPENDENT visual design critic with strong taste. You do NOT edit code. Using your Playwright MCP browser tools, OPEN the in-cluster preview URL from the prompt — this serves the PUBLIC sign-in page. IMPORTANT: it is a DEV server that compiles SSR on first load, so the FIRST navigation can take 1-3 MINUTES — set a generous navigation timeout (e.g. 180000 ms) and if it times out, RETRY the navigation once or twice before giving up; wait until the page has actually rendered. Then ALWAYS take a screenshot (browser_take_screenshot) of the rendered page (this also records the review video), and inspect it: judge the sign-in page against the stated intent on visual hierarchy & clarity, cohesion (type scale, spacing rhythm, color harmony, AA contrast), distinctiveness (not a generic/AI-default look), and that the sign-in form is intact. CALIBRATION: a PASS means the intent is genuinely well-realized and the page is crafted, not merely 'fine'; DEFAULT TO NOT satisfied unless clearly strong; cite concrete visible specifics. If you could not get the page to render at all after retries, return meets_criteria:false with feedback saying the preview did not load. You MUST end your turn with ONLY a single strict JSON object, no prose, no fences: {\"meets_criteria\": boolean, \"score\": number 0-10, \"feedback\": string}.

body.prompt:
${ "Review the LIVE sign-in page against this change request using your Playwright browser tools.

Change request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the sign-in page") + "

Open the in-cluster preview URL: " + (.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + " (it serves the sign-in page at /). The FIRST load is slow (dev SSR compile) — use a 180000 ms navigation timeout and retry once or twice if needed; wait for it to render, then ALWAYS browser_take_screenshot before judging. Return ONLY the strict JSON verdict {meets_criteria, score, feedback}. Be demanding: meets_criteria true ONLY if the intent is clearly well-realized AND the form still works." }

artifacts.title: In-preview GAN result
artifacts.description: Accepted flag, iteration count, final generator summary, and the critic's last verdict.
